factual_core: Design history and art history overlap but are not identical: design foregrounds use, communication, reproduction, systems, and clients; art history often foregrounds works, institutions, medium, authorship, and reception.
short_intro: A relation card for separating design and art without reducing either side.
entry_points: compare use, medium, institution, audience, and reproduction

conversation_moves.overview: Separate design history from art history by function, medium, institution, and audience.
conversation_moves.entry_path: Start with Bauhaus, modernism, and museum/design institution differences.
conversation_moves.explain_work: Apply to a named object if supplied.
conversation_moves.compare: Mention both art/design and at least one comparison axis.
conversation_moves.why_it_matters: Prevents art/design questions from collapsing to one field.

copyright_policy: Use abstract comparison only; no long quoted source text.
source_summary: Public general design/art history knowledge abstracted into a relation card.
boundary_notes: Not active as public semantic content.

factual_core: Heidegger and Sartre can be compared through being, existence, freedom, subjectivity, and responsibility; Sartre is more directly tied to existentialist ethics and freedom.
short_intro: A relation card for distinguishing ontology and existentialist human freedom.
entry_points: compare being, freedom, responsibility, and subjectivity

conversation_moves.overview: Distinguish Heidegger and Sartre without reducing both to one slogan.
conversation_moves.explain_work: Apply to a named text if carded.
conversation_moves.compare: Mention both sides and at least one axis.
conversation_moves.why_it_matters: Separates related but different philosophical projects.

safe_boundaries: do not claim identity between schools
copyright_policy: No long excerpts; summarize concepts only.
source_summary: Public general philosophy knowledge abstracted into a relation card.
boundary_notes: Not active as public semantic content.

names: 中国现代文学和日本近代文学
中国现代文学和日本文学
factual_core: Chinese modern literature and Japanese modern literature can be compared through modernization, language reform, social critique, subject formation, translation, and different national histories.
short_intro: A cross-East-Asian relation card for comparison without collapsing Asian literature to Japan.
entry_points: compare modernization, language, social critique, and subject formation

conversation_moves.overview: Use China/Japan as related traditions, not one merged tradition.
conversation_moves.representative_works: Name concrete works only when asked.
conversation_moves.entry_path: Start with Lu Xun/Soseki and Zhang/Kawabata relation cards.
conversation_moves.explain_work: Apply to a specific work if supplied.
conversation_moves.compare: Mention both China and Japan plus at least one axis.
conversation_moves.country_relation: Separate country history from literary tradition.
conversation_moves.why_it_matters: Prevents Asian literature from collapsing to one anchor.

copyright_policy: No long excerpts; use metadata and abstract comparison.
source_summary: Public general literary-historical knowledge abstracted into a relation card.

provenance.label: Britannica:中国现代文学和日本文学

boundary_notes: Not active as public semantic content.
